title: aquilegia discolor subsp. reuteri
intro: Aquilegia discolor subsp. reuteri is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Columbine plant, a flowering perennial native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Aquilegia discolor subsp. reuteri can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which may be used to harness its medicinal properties.
